file.content:
{
  "name": "neuman-brain",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Neuman Brain - Privacy-first, local-only productivity dashboard",
  "author": "Travis Neuman",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "build:production": "NODE_ENV=production npm run build",
    "lint": "eslint . --config config/eslint.config.js",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:ui": "vitest --ui --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "test:e2e:chromium": "playwright test --project=chromium",
    "test:e2e:firefox": "playwright test --project=firefox",
    "test:e2e:webkit": "playwright test --project=webkit",
    "test:e2e:report": "playwright show-report",
    "type-check": "tsc -b --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.69.0",
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@google/generative-ai": "^0.24.1",
    "@huggingface/inference": "^4.13.3",
    "@lexical/code": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/link": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/list": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/markdown": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/react": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/rich-text": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/table": "^0.38.2",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.56.1",
    "@types/crypto-js": "^4.2.2",
    "@types/d3": "^7.4.3",
    "@webcontainer/api": "^1.6.1",
    "@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.10.0",
    "@xterm/addon-search": "^0.15.0",
    "@xterm/addon-web-links": "^0.11.0",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
    "chrono-node": "^2.9.0",
    "crypto-js": "^4.2.0",
    "d3": "^7.9.0",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "dexie": "^4.2.1",
    "framer-motion": "^12.23.24",
    "groq-sdk": "^0.35.0",
    "html2canvas": "^1.4.1",
    "ics": "^3.8.1",
    "jspdf": "^3.0.4",
    "jszip": "^3.10.1",
    "konva": "^10.0.12",
    "leaflet": "^1.9.4",
    "lexical": "^0.38.2",
    "lucide-react": "^0.554.0",
    "openai": "^6.9.0",
    "react": "^19.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.0",
    "react-konva": "^19.2.1",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-router-dom": "^7.9.5",
    "recharts": "^3.5.0",
    "rehype-sanitize": "^6.0.0",
    "roughjs": "^4.6.6",
    "uuid": "^13.0.0",
    "xlsx": "^0.18.5",
    "zod": "^4.1.13",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.1",
    "@testing-library/jest-dom": "^6.9.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "^16.3.0",
    "@testing-library/user-event": "^14.6.1",
    "@types/canvas-confetti": "^1.9.0",
    "@types/html2canvas": "^0.5.35",
    "@types/jspdf": "^1.3.3",
    "@types/leaflet": "^1.9.21",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.2",
    "@types/react-grid-layout": "^1.3.5",
    "@types/uuid": "^10.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.1.0",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.21",
    "canvas-confetti": "^1.9.4",
    "eslint": "^9.39.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^5.2.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.24",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "jsdom": "^27.1.0",
    "postcss": "^8.5.6",
    "rollup-plugin-visualizer": "^6.0.5",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.18",
    "typescript": "~5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.46.3",
    "vite": "^7.2.2",
    "vitest": "^4.0.8"
  }
}

content.text: Now let me check the CommandPalette types and searchRegistry for more context on how shortcuts are handled:

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Synapse Types
     3→ *
     4→ * Type definitions for Synapse - Neuman Brain's neural search interface.
     5→ * Provides quick access to all platform data via Ctrl+K / Cmd+K.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import type { ReactNode } from 'react';
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * Result types that can appear in search results
    12→ */
    13→export type SearchResultType =
    14→  | 'note'
    15→  | 'task'
    16→  | 'event'
    17→  | 'bookmark'
    18→  | 'page'
    19→  | 'action'
    20→  | 'external'
    21→  | 'diagram'
    22→  | 'form'
    23→  | 'time-entry'
    24→  | 'faq'
    25→  | 'help'
    26→  | 'widget'
    27→  | 'setting'
    28→  | 'automation'
    29→  | 'template'
    30→  | 'project'
    31→  | 'shortcut';
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Search result item displayed in the command palette
    35→ */
    36→export interface SearchResult {
    37→  /** Unique identifier for this result */
    38→  id: string;
    39→  /** Type of result for categorization and icon selection */
    40→  type: SearchResultType;
    41→  /** Primary display text */
    42→  title: string;
    43→  /** Secondary text (path, description, URL, etc.) */
    44→  subtitle?: string;
    45→  /** Icon to display (emoji, Lucide component, or custom ReactNode) */
    46→  icon?: string | ReactNode;
    47→  /** Relevance score for sorting (higher = more relevant) */
    48→  score: number;
    49→  /** Action to execute when selected */
    50→  action: () => void;
    51→  /** Optional preview content */
    52→  preview?: string;
    53→  /** Additional metadata for filtering/display */
    54→  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
    55→  /** Keywords for fuzzy matching (in addition to title/subtitle) */
    56→  keywords?: string[];
    57→}
    58→
    59→/**
    60→ * Search source configuration for dynamic data registration
    61→ */
    62→export interface SearchSource {
    63→  /** Unique identifier for this source */
    64→  id: string;
    65→  /** Display name for the source */
    66→  name: string;
    67→  /** Result type produced by this source */
    68→  type: SearchResultType;
    69→  /** Icon for results from this source */
    70→  icon: string | ReactNode;
    71→  /** Function that returns all searchable items */
    72→  getItems: () => SearchResult[];
    73→  /** Priority for ordering sources (higher = shown first) */
    74→  priority?: number;
    75→  /** Whether this source is enabled */
    76→  enabled?: boolean;
    77→}
    78→

    82→export interface SearchEngine {

    93→  /** Keyboard shortcut hint (optional) */

    95→}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive summary. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary: Keyboard Shortcut Implementation in Neuman Brain

### Current Keyboard Shortcut Implementation

#### 1. **Global Shortcuts (Layout.tsx - Lines 63-103)**
Currently implemented at the application root level via `useEffect` with capture-phase event listeners:

**Active Global Shortcuts:**
- **Cmd+K / Ctrl+K**: Opens CommandPalette (Synapse - neural search)
- **F1**: Opens Help/Support modal
- **Cmd+/ / Ctrl+/**: Opens Help/Support modal  
- **Cmd+Shift+P / Ctrl+Shift+P**: Toggles project context dropdown

**Implementation Pattern:**
```typescript
useEffect(() => {
  const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
    if (e.key === 'k' && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) {
      e.preventDefault();
      e.stopPropagation();
      setShowCommandPalette(true);
    }
    // ... more shortcuts
  };
  
  window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, { capture: true });
  return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, { capture: true });
}, [toggleProjectDropdown]);
```

---

#### 2. **Context-Specific Shortcuts**

**Notes Page (Notes.tsx):**
- **Cmd+K / Ctrl+K**: Focus search input (line 218-229)
- **Cmd+Shift+E / Ctrl+Shift+E**: Open export modal (line 1083-1093)

**Kanban Board (useKanbanKeyboard.ts hook):**
- **N**: New task in current column
- **J/K**: Navigate down/up through tasks (Vim-style)
- **H/L**: Navigate left/right through columns (Vim-style)
- **E**: Edit selected task
- **D**: Delete selected task (with confirmation)
- **?**: Toggle keyboard help
- **Esc**: Clear selection

**Diagram Editor (DiagramEditor.tsx):**
- **Cmd+A / Ctrl+A**: Select all elements
- **Esc**: Clear selection
- **Delete**: Delete selected elements

**Natural Language Date Input (NaturalLanguageDateInput.tsx):**
- **Cmd+K / Ctrl+K**: Clear date field

---

#### 3. **Keyboard Navigation Hooks**

**useEscapeKey.ts** (Lines 1-53):
- Handles Escape key with priority system to prevent conflicts
- Used by modals with configurable priority levels (z-index based)
- Example: CommandPalette uses priority 10000 to intercept all Escape events first
- Uses capture phase and stores priority on event object

**useKeyboardNavigation.ts**:
- Arrow Up/Down: Navigate list items
- Enter: Select item
- Escape: Cancel/close
- Supports wrapping at boundaries
- Used in CommandPalette search results

---

#### 4. **Special Cases**

**Lexical Editor (NotesEditor.tsx):**
- Uses `@lexical/react/LexicalMarkdownShortcutPlugin` for markdown shortcuts
- Supports slash commands (via Lexical's built-in system)
- Keyboard events managed internally by Lexical

**Inline Date Shortcuts (useInlineDateShortcuts.ts):**
- Text pattern matching on input: `@today`, `@tomorrow`, `+3d`, `+2w`, etc.
- NOT keyboard shortcuts, but text shortcuts (input event based)
- Configurable via settings

---

### Implementation Patterns

#### **Pattern 1: Direct Window Event Listeners**
Used in Layout.tsx, Notes.tsx, DiagramEditor.tsx:
```typescript
useEffect(() => {
  const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
    if (e.key === 'k' && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) {
      e.preventDefault();
      setShowModal(true);
    }
  };
  window.addEventListener('keydown', handler, { capture: true });
  return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handler, { capture: true });
}, [dependencies]);
```

**Pros:** Simple, works across all pages
**Cons:** Multiple event listeners on window, potential conflicts, hard to manage at scale

#### **Pattern 2: Custom Hook Wrappers**
Used for common patterns (useEscapeKey, useKeyboardNavigation, useKanbanKeyboard):
```typescript
export function useEscapeKey({ enabled, onEscape, priority }: Options) {
  useEffect(() => {
    const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
        const currentPriority = (e as any)._escPriority || -1;
        if (priority > currentPriority) {
          (e as any)._escPriority = priority;
          e.stopPropagation();
          e.preventDefault();
          onEscape();
        }
      }
    };
    document.addEventListener('keydown', handler, { capture: true });
    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler, { capture: true });
  }, [enabled, onEscape, priority]);
}
```

**Pros:** Reusable, conflict handling via priority system
**Cons:** Only handles specific keys (Escape, arrow keys, etc.)

#### **Pattern 3: Component-Level Event Handlers**
Used in CommandPalette.tsx:
```typescript
const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
  (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
    switch (e.key) {
      case 'ArrowDown':
        e.preventDefault();
        setSelectedIndex(prev => (prev + 1) % items.length);
        break;
      // ...
    }
  },
  [flatResults, selectedIndex, onClose]
);

<input onKeyDown={handleKeyDown} />
```

**Pros:** Scoped to component, no global pollution
**Cons:** Only works for focused input, not global shortcuts

### Considerations for Global Shortcuts System

#### **Conflicts to Watch For:**
1. **Multiple useEffect listeners on window** - Both Layout.tsx (global) and individual pages (Notes.tsx, DiagramEditor.tsx) register keydown handlers
2. **Capture vs. Bubble phase** - useEscapeKey uses capture phase; most others use default (bubble)
3. **Preventing input field triggers** - useKanbanKeyboard checks `e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement`
4. **Modal priority collision** - useEscapeKey stores priority on event object (could cause race conditions with other systems)
5. **Cmd vs. Ctrl platform detection** - All shortcuts use `(e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)` pattern (Mac vs. Windows)

#### **No Existing Dependencies:**
- ✅ No hotkey libraries (react-hotkeys, hotkeys-js, etc.)
- ✅ No centralized shortcut registry
- ✅ No conflict resolution system (except useEscapeKey priority hack)
- ✅ No shortcut customization/remapping UI

#### **Documentation Exists:**
- KeyboardShortcutsHelp.tsx - Kanban-specific help modal
- ShortcutsWidget.tsx - Dashboard widget (hardcoded, static reference)
- searchRegistry.ts - Mentions shortcuts in searchable help (line 816)

---

1. **`/src/hooks/`** - Create new hook(s):
   - `useGlobalShortcuts.ts` - Central shortcut registration & dispatch
   - `useShortcutRegistry.ts` - Registry management (optional)

2. **`/src/stores/`** - Create store:
   - `useShortcutsStore.ts` - Centralized shortcut state & registry

3. **`/src/components/Layout.tsx`** - Replace inline keyboard handling with hook

4. **`/src/services/`** - Create service:
   - `shortcuts.ts` - Shortcut definition constants & utilities

5. **Update existing components**:
   - `/src/pages/Notes.tsx` - Replace inline useEffect
   - `/src/pages/DiagramEditor.tsx` - Replace inline useEffect
   - `/src/components/CommandPalette/CommandPalette.tsx` - Consider integration

6. **Documentation**:
   - Create `/docs/technical/keyboard_shortcuts_guide.md` - Usage & architecture

---

### Architecture Recommendation

For a scalable global shortcut system:

1. **Central Registry Store** (`useShortcutsStore.ts`):
   - Register/unregister shortcuts at runtime
   - Store active shortcuts with metadata (name, description, keys, handler)
   - Support context-scoped shortcuts (enabled only on specific pages)

2. **Global Listener Hook** (`useGlobalShortcuts.ts`):
   - Single window keydown listener attached at app root
   - Dispatches to registered handlers based on current context
   - Handles modifier key combinations intelligently

3. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Priority system (modal > page > global)
   - Context awareness (only match shortcuts for active page/component)
   - Prevention rules (don't trigger in input/textarea)

4. **Configuration**:
   - Centralized shortcut definitions in `/src/services/shortcuts.ts`
   - Settings-driven customization (optional remapping)
   - Help modal auto-generated from registry

This would consolidate the ~5 separate useEffect listeners currently scattered across the codebase into a single, managed system.
